data.title: What's Going On With Kyndryl Stock Friday?
data.description:
Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:KD) stock is trading flat in Friday’s premarket as the company attempts to stabilize following a tumultuous week.

While Kyndryl recently secured a major modernization contract in Japan, the news is being weighed down by a widening accounting review and a sudden leadership exodus.

Yamaguchi Collaboration

Kyndryl announced its collaboration with Yamaguchi Financial Group to build an integrated platform for its core banking system, which will allow three banks to operate on a shared IT infrastructure.

The initiative, which began in January 2026, is targeted for launch in January 2029, enhancing IT management and reducing operational costs.

The new system will consolidate application programs into a unified environment, improving development productivity. Kyndryl Japan’s extensive experience in supporting mission-critical systems in the financial services sector is expected to play a crucial role in this transformation.

Governance Turmoil & SEC Investigation

On February 9, Kyndryl revealed that its Audit Committee is reviewing cash-management practices and internal controls after receiving voluntary document requests from the SEC’s Division of Enforcement.

The governance crisis has been compounded by a swift leadership shakeup, including the departures of CFO David Wyshner and General Counsel Edward Sebold.

Kyndryl also sharply lowered its fiscal 2026 outlook, slashing its adjusted pretax income guidance to $575 million–$600 million from the previously projected $725 million. Free cash flow expectations were also pared back significantly to $325 million–$375 million, nearly $200 million below prior estimates.

Analysts have taken a cautious stance, with JPMorgan downgrading the stock to Underweight and setting a $16 price forecast, citing concerns over the unexpected CFO departure and the delayed filing of the company’s 10-Q.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 47.5% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 57.3% below its 100-day SMA, indicating significant short-term weakness. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 72.70% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 22.16, which is considered oversold territory, suggesting that the stock may be undervalued. Meanwhile, MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of oversold RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock is oversold, there is still downward pressure.

  • Key Resistance: $21.50
  • Key Support: $10.00

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. is slated to provide its next financial update on May 6, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: 70 cents (Up from 52 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $4.08 billion (Up from $3.80 billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 10.8x (Indicates value opportunity)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $36.00. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Guggenheim: Downgraded to Neutral (Feb. 10)

Valuation Insight: While the stock trades at a value P/E multiple, the strong consensus and rising estimates suggest analysts view the growth prospects as justification for the 217% upside to analyst targets.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Kyndryl Holdings, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 63.86 — The stock shows a moderate value ranking, indicating potential for investment.
  • Momentum: 0.8 — The low momentum score suggests the stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Kyndryl Holdings’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the value score indicates some potential for investment, the weak momentum score suggests caution for investors considering entry points.

ETF Exposure

  • Inspire Faithward Mid Cap Momentum ETF (NYSE:GLRY): 3.39% Weight
  • Russell US Small Cap Equity Active ETF (NASDAQ:RUSC): 0.43% Weight

Significance: Because KD carries meaningful weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

KD Price Action: Kyndryl Hldgs shares were up 0.18% at $11.36 during premarket trading on Friday. The stock is near its 52-week low of $10.10, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Top 3 Defensive Stocks That May Rocket Higher In February
data.description:
The most oversold stocks in the consumer staples sector presents an opportunity to buy into undervalued companies.

The RSI is a momentum indicator, which compares a stock’s strength on days when prices go up to its strength on days when prices go down. When compared to a stock’s price action, it can give traders a better sense of how a stock may perform in the short term. An asset is typically considered oversold when the RSI is below 30, according to Benzinga Pro.

Here's the latest list of major oversold players in this sector, having an RSI near or below 30.

data.title: Nu Skin Stock Sinks After Weak Quarter, Soft Outlook
data.description:
The company missed analyst expectations, reporting GAAP diluted EPS of 29 cents compared to a 30-cent estimate, and revenue of $370.32 million versus an expected $382.70 million.

Revenue declined 16.9% from $445.55 million a year ago. EPS, excluding restructuring and other charges, was 29 cents compared to 38 cents last year.

Gross margin increased to 70.7% from 62.7%. Operating margin improved to 6.3% from a negative 11.9%. The customer base totaled 748,796, down 10%. Paid affiliates numbered 129,311, a decrease of 11%, and sales leaders totaled 30,045, down 19%.

The company said more than 20,000 Prysm iO devices are already in the hands of its sales leaders, generating more than 700,000 scans. It has begun pre-market activities in India ahead of a full market opening anticipated in the back half of the year.

By segment, Nu Skin revenue was $321.80 million, down from $362.45 million. Rhyz’s revenue was $48.52 million, down from $83.10 million.

Nu Skin ended 2025 with $238.63 million in cash and cash equivalents. Total debt was $224.19 million, of which $20.0 million was due within one year.

Ryan Napierski, Nu Skin president and CEO, commented, “This past year was a pivotal year as we furthered our transformation toward becoming the world’s leading intelligent beauty and wellness platform and laid the groundwork for our 2026 growth initiatives.”

“Looking ahead, we continue to build sales leader alignment in the first half of the year around the global launch of Prysm iO, positioning us for a return to year-over-year revenue growth by year’s end.”

Outlook

For the first quarter of 2026, Nu Skin forecasts revenue between $320.00 million and $340.00 million, below the $356.80 million estimate. The company expects GAAP EPS of 10 to 20 cents, compared to a 27-cent estimate.

For full-year 2026, the company projects revenue between $1.35 billion and $1.50 billion, compared to a $1.53 billion estimate. GAAP EPS is expected to range from 80 cents to $1.20, versus a $1.42 estimate.

NUS Price Action: Nu Skin Enterprises shares were down 16.75% at $8.50 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Baidu Embeds OpenClaw In Search App Ahead Of Lunar New Year
data.description:
Baidu Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) is embedding the fast-rising OpenClaw AI agent directly into its flagship search app, opening the tool to roughly 700 million monthly users just days before Lunar New Year.

  • BIDU is consolidating close to its 52-week peak. See real-time price here.

The move escalates China’s AI arms race, positioning Baidu to capture fresh user engagement and monetize automation features at scale as rivals roll out competing integrations.

This integration gives Baidu’s vast user base new ways to apply AI in everyday tasks, CNBC reported on Friday.

How OpenClaw Revolutionizes User Interactions

Previously, OpenClaw was accessible only through chat-based platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram. Now, users can use this AI directly within Baidu’s app, streamlining tasks such as coding and managing email.

This move is part of a broader strategy where Baidu is also deploying OpenClaw’s features across its e-commerce and other digital services. The integration aims to capitalize on the growing demand for AI-driven solutions.

Are Chinese Tech Giants Battling For AI Supremacy?

These companies are in a race to attract new users and monetize their AI investments, particularly ahead of major holidays like the Lunar New Year, which presents a lucrative opportunity for user engagement and revenue growth, the report added.

Market Trends Indicating A Surge In AI Integration

BIDU Price Action: Baidu shares were up 0.09% at $138.50 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image by bluestork via Shutterstock
